title: All service ports match canonical registry — no collisions
evidence: service-ports.json: coordinator=3000, P1=3001, P2=3002, P3=3003, P4=3004, EE=3005, CC=3006, UD=3007, MD=3008, monolith=3100, coord-worker=3009. Verified against: cross-chain-detector/index.ts:25 (3006), execution-engine/index.ts:50 (3005), coordinator/index.ts:72 (3000), monolith/index.ts:65 (3100), unified-detector/constants.ts:67 (3007). Monolith worker assignments (index.ts:107-173) match registry for all sub-services.
actual: All 11 service port assignments match the canonical registry. No collisions detected.

title: parseStreamResult empty catch on JSON.parse fallback — intentional pattern
evidence: shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:1487 — `catch { data[key] = value; }` silently falls back to raw string when JSON.parse fails.
expected: Parse failures should be logged or counted
actual: Silent fallback to raw string. This is intentional for Redis stream fields that may be plain strings (not JSON-wrapped). Most occurrences are expected (e.g., stream IDs, plain string values). Not a new finding — present in previous session as well.
recommendation: LOW — Known pattern. No change from prior session.

title: No new silent error swallowing in changed files
evidence: Audited all 28 changed files for catch blocks. coordinator.ts: 16 catch blocks, all log before return/rethrow. monolith/worker-manager.ts: 3 catch blocks, all log. shared/core/redis/client.ts: 30+ catch blocks, all log or rethrow (ping() correctly returns false). shared/core/redis/streams.ts: 18+ catch blocks, all log or rethrow. shared/security/rate-limiter.ts: 4 catch blocks, all log. Factory parsers (7 files): all have console.debug logging (SA-3M-001 fix applied). multi-leg-path-finder.ts:632 has logger.debug (SA-3M-002 fix applied).
expected: No new empty catch blocks in changed files
actual: Zero new silent error swallowing introduced. Previous SA-3M-001 and SA-3M-002 fixes confirmed applied — all factory parsers now log with console.debug, multi-leg-path-finder now logs with logger.debug.
recommendation: None required. Good error handling across all changed files.

title: Previous SA-3N-001 and SA-3N-004 RESOLVED — all 9 numeric fields now restored in validation.ts
evidence: validation.ts:332-363 now restores ALL numeric fields: profitPercentage (L333), confidence (L336), expectedProfit (L339), estimatedProfit (L342), buyPrice (L347), sellPrice (L350), blockNumber (L353, parseInt), timestamp (L358, Number()), expiresAt (L362, Number()). Comments at L344 and L355-356 document the SA-3N-004 fix.
expected: All numeric fields restored from string after Redis deserialization
actual: 9 of 9 numeric fields now explicitly restored to their correct types. Previous findings SA-3N-001 (timestamp/expiresAt not restored) and SA-3N-004 (buyPrice/sellPrice/blockNumber not restored) are fully remediated.
recommendation: None required. Excellent type restoration coverage.

title: as unknown as ArbitrageOpportunity cast remains but is now justified by thorough field restoration
evidence: validation.ts:367 — `data as unknown as ArbitrageOpportunity`. The cast follows validation of required fields (id, type, tokenIn, tokenOut, amountIn) and explicit numeric restoration of 9 fields.
expected: Cast acceptable when preceded by validation
actual: The cast is now well-justified: all required fields are structurally validated, and all numeric fields are explicitly restored. The remaining unvalidated fields (chain, dexIn, dexOut, path, gasEstimate, etc.) are optional in the ArbitrageOpportunity interface and used conditionally downstream.
recommendation: LOW — No runtime risk. A full deserializer function would be ideal for type safety but is not required given the current validation coverage.

title: stream-serialization.ts unchanged — serialization/deserialization pair in sync
evidence: changed-files.txt does not include services/coordinator/src/utils/stream-serialization.ts. Only the deserialization side (validation.ts at L332-363 and streams.ts parseStreamResult at L1437-1497) was in changed files.
expected: Serialization and deserialization should remain in sync
actual: Serialization side unchanged. Deserialization side (validation.ts) now restores all 9 fields that serialization converts to strings. The pair is in sync.
